findings.description: Digital signature metadata rendered as visible text in HTML, not visible in the PDF rendering. This inserts a block of extraneous content (DN, organization, email, timestamp) between the 'From' field and the 'Subject' field, disrupting reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'From: Elizabeth S. Werner / Division of Waste Management / Raleigh Central Office' followed directly by 'Subject: Meeting Officer's Report'. HTML inserts a visible block: 'Digitally signed by Elizabeth S Werner / DN: cn=Elizabeth S Werner, o=DWM-Soild Waste, ou=NCDENR, email=elizabeth.werner@ncdenr.gov, c=US / Date: 2016.04.28 10:23:05 -04'00'' between From and Subject.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of Elizabeth S. Werner visible in the PDF (page 1, next to the digital signature stamp) has no corresponding tag in the HTML. Signatures are explicitly listed as substantive images.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a cursive/handwritten signature reading 'Elizabeth S. Werner' adjacent to the digital signature overlay. The HTML has no for this signature; only the digital certificate metadata text is reproduced as a plain paragraph.

findings.description: The alt text for the NC logo image describes a 'pine tree icon,' which does not clearly correspond to the actual logo design visible in the PDF. The logo shows a stylized 'NC' in blue and red with the words 'Waste Management' and 'ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY.'
findings.evidence: HTML alt text: 'North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality Waste Management logo with a pine tree icon.' The PDF logo does not clearly contain a pine tree — it primarily features a stylized 'NC' letterform with agency text.
